title: epiphyllum hookeri subsp. guatemalense
intro: Epiphyllum hookeri subsp. guatemalense, also known as the Guatemalan epiphyllum, is a type of flowering epiphyte native to Central America, characterized by its long, thin stems and fragrant, nocturnal flowers. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Epiphyllum hookeri subsp. guatemalense are attributed to its ability to reduce blood pressure, improve respiratory function, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to promote relaxation, soothe digestive issues, and support overall well-being.
